description: Without decoration or elaborate features; simple and unembellished. 'Mueli' names the quality of plainness as aesthetic choice—beauty in simplicity, sufficiency without ornament.
sound_symbolism: The word rests simply. 'Mu' grounds with quiet presence, nothing added. The vowel 'e' opens to clarity, decoration absent. Then 'li' settles lightly, plainness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'mueli thae' (plain bowl). Distinct from 'wemo' (plain as flat land). Related to 'keruko' (sturdy) and 'siloma' (simple) but emphasizes absence of decoration.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Plain living supports clarity—simplicity in material life reduces distraction and craving.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Plain surfaces created visual rest—even ornate designs needed undecorated spaces to breathe.
